summary: Russia claims its forces liberated Novoselovatoe in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, while Russian MoD reported shooting down 3 FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles and 511 Ukrainian drones in a 24-hour period. A major prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian military personnel home, with Ukraine also recovering seven civilians illegally detained since 2022. Ukrainian forces struck the FNVTs Titan-Barrikady defense plant in Volgograd and the Vtorovo petroleum pipeline station in Vladimir Oblast, Russia, as fuel queues reportedly formed across Russian cities.
key_points: Russian MoD claims forces liberated the settlement of Novoselovatoe in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382637)
Russian air defenses claim to have shot down 3 FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles and 511 Ukrainian aircraft-type drones in 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382639)
Ukraine lost a MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast overnight on June 27, 2026; the pilot ejected safely and was hospitalized. (Source: @eRadarrua/98367)
Ukrainian forces struck the Titan-Barrikady defense plant in Volgograd, which produces launchers and components for Iskander-M, Yars, and Topol-M systems. (Source: @eRadarrua/98338)
SBU struck the Vtorovo pipeline dispatch station in Vladimir Oblast, Russia, described as a key logistics node for light petroleum products. (Source: @UkraineNow/82220)
A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian military personnel home; over 9,500 total have been returned since the start of the full-scale war. (Source: @UkraineNow/82206)
Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Syrsky stated Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by year's end. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193)
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated Belarus is not expected to enter the war. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191)

sections.executive_summary: Russia claims its forces liberated Novoselovatoe in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast, while Russian MoD reported shooting down 3 FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles and 511 Ukrainian drones in a 24-hour period. A major prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian military personnel home, with Ukraine also recovering seven civilians illegally detained since 2022. Ukrainian forces struck the FNVTs Titan-Barrikady defense plant in Volgograd and the Vtorovo petroleum pipeline station in Vladimir Oblast, Russia, as fuel queues reportedly formed across Russian cities.

points: A Ukrainian MiG-29 was lost over Poltava Oblast overnight on June 27, 2026 while conducting a combat mission; the pilot successfully ejected, contacted a search-and-rescue team, and was taken to a medical facility. The cause is under investigation. (Source: @eRadarrua/98367)

Ukraine and Russia conducted a prisoner exchange returning 160 Ukrainian military personnel home. President Zelensky stated over 9,500 people have been returned since the start of the full-scale war, with 1,596 returned this year alone. (Source: @UkraineNow/82206)

Seven Ukrainian civilians aged 35-66, illegally detained by Russia since 2022 during the occupation of Mariupol, Kyiv, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, and Luhansk oblasts, were returned home. One was taken from his home because his son serves in the AFU. (Source: @UkraineNow/82226)

Ukrainian forces struck the FNVTs Titan-Barrikady plant in Volgograd, a defense enterprise that produces launchers, artillery systems, and components for Iskander-M, Yars, and Topol-M missile systems. (Source: @eRadarrua/98338)

SBU struck the Vtorovo linear-production-dispatch station in Vladimir Oblast, Russia for the second time in a month. The station is described as a key logistics node for pumping light petroleum products to export ports and domestic consumers. (Source: @UkraineNow/82220)
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga stated that Belarus is not expected to enter the war, saying Ukraine would mirror any attempt by Russia to drag Belarus in. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191)

Ukraine's air command issued warnings of tactical aviation threat on northeastern and southeastern directions, with groups of strike UAVs heading toward Mykolaiv Oblast from the south and reactive BPLAs crossing Sumy toward Poltava. (Source: @kpszsu/66541)
Russian MoD claims forces liberated the settlement of Novoselovatoe in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast. (Source: @tass_agency/382637)
Russian air defenses claim to have shot down 3 FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles and 511 Ukrainian aircraft-type drones over the past 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382639)

Seven UAVs heading for Moscow were destroyed overnight; Sevastopol, Crimea, and Sochi also repelled attacks. From 7:00 AM to 8:00 PM, 175 drones were recorded shot down. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
The governor of Volgograd reported that production facilities of an enterprise in the Krasnooktyabrsky district were damaged, with ten injured reported. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Russian Aerospace Forces dropped nine FAB-500 aerial bombs on Ukrainian drone control centers in DPR, Dnipropetrovsk, and Sumy regions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31186)

Russian forces are intensifying strikes against locomotives with remotely controlled Geraniums, destroying 2 under a bridge in Zaporizhzhia and 3 in Kharkov region, and targeting gas stations. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Traffic on the Crimean Bridge was restored after a prior disruption. (Source: @tass_agency/382617)

In the Kharkov direction, Army Group North continues offensive operations in Kazachya Lopan and adjacent forest areas; enemy counterattacks were repelled and prisoners taken. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Vovchansk sector, small-arms fighting continues in Losevka, Ukrainske, and Zemlyanoy Yar; the enemy made an unsuccessful counterattack attempt with several battle groups in Zemlyanoy Yar. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
On the Velyky Burluk sector, Russian forces are fighting in Petro-Ivanovka. South of Kupyansk, forces are fighting in the area of Kovsharovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
In Krasny Liman, storming of the city continues; Russian forces are destroying approaching enemy reserves and striking crossings over the Oskol River. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Slavyansk direction, Russian forces continue offensive operations on a broad front from Ray-Aleksandrovka. Storming of Konstantinovka continues with success in urban areas; FAB strikes shifted to the near rear of AFU in Druzhkovka. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In the Dnipropetrovsk region, Army Group East captured strongholds on the Velykomihailovka-Alexandrovka line; enemy counterattack with three armored combat vehicles against Trans-Baikal troops was unsuccessful. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In eastern Zaporizhzhie, Far Eastern forces continued wedging into enemy defense west of Vozdvyzhevka, Verkhnya Tersa, and Komsomolske, forcing the enemy to commit reserves on multiple sectors. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

On the Sumy direction, in the Shostka district, assault troops of Army Group North continue small-arms battles in Bachevsk and surroundings. In Sumy district, fighting in Pysarevka and Novaya Sech. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
In Kursk region, Belovsky district, an enemy UAV attacked a grocery store in Belaya, wounding a woman; another person was injured in the village of Giri. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)

In Belgorod region, Shebekino, a Ukrainian FPV drone detonated at an enterprise, injuring a civilian. A 15-year-old boy was injured by an FPV drone in Nikolsky khutor. A man was killed by a munition detonation in Ploskoye, Korochansky district. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Five residents of Kursk region captured by Ukraine in 2024 returned home; 171 residents of Kursk border areas have been returned from Ukrainian territory in total. (Source: @tass_agency/382643)
A reactive BPLA was detected crossing Sumy region toward Bila Poltava, and another BPLAs group was reported between Bohodukhov and Vilsany in Kharkiv Oblast. (Source: @kpszsu/66562)
A high-speed target crossed Sumy region toward Poltava, and a threat of strike UAV use was declared for Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia oblasts. (Source: @kpszsu/66530)

In Zaporizhzhie region, 18 strikes on settlements were recorded over the previous 24 hours with one killed and four injured. The AFU continued strikes on infrastructure of the Zaporizhzhia NPP. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
In Kherson region, Sofiyevka (Kakhovsky district), a boy born in 2010 was injured. In Kakhovka, a man and a woman were wounded. In Dnipryany, a civilian was wounded. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
In Kakhovsky district, an enemy drone deliberately struck an ambulance; a female paramedic was killed and the driver was seriously injured. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
BPLAs were reported over Zaporizhzhia, and a group of reactive BPLAs was heading toward Mykolaiv Oblast from the south. (Source: @kpszsu/66549)
Tactical aviation activity was reported on the northeastern direction with a threat of aviation strike means for frontline oblasts. (Source: @kpszsu/66557)
Ukraine lost a MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast overnight June 27, 2026; pilot ejected safely and was hospitalized. Cause under investigation. (Source: @eRadarrua/98367)
Russian MoD claims 3 FP-5 Flamingo cruise missiles and 511 Ukrainian aircraft-type drones shot down in 24 hours. (Source: @tass_agency/382639)
Seven UAVs heading for Moscow destroyed overnight; 175 drones recorded shot down between 7:00 AM and 8:00 PM. Sevastopol and Sochi also repelled attacks. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31204)
Russian Aerospace Forces dropped nine FAB-500 bombs on Ukrainian drone control centers in DPR, Dnipropetrovsk, and Sumy regions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31186)
Ukrainian forces struck the Titan-Barrikady defense plant in Volgograd with FP-5 Flamingo missiles; the governor reported ten injured and damage to production facilities. (Source: @eRadarrua/98338)
A MiG-31K, carrier of the Kh-47M2 Kinzhal aeroballistic missile, flew from Savaysleyka airfield, prompting air raid alerts. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68081)

Warnings issued for tactical aviation activity on northeastern and southeastern directions, with strike UAVs heading toward Mykolaiv Oblast and reactive BPLAs crossing Sumy toward Poltava. (Source: @kpszsu/66541)

key_developments: Russian forces claim capture of Novoselovatoe in Dnipropetrovsk Oblast as Army Group East also seized strongholds on the Velykomihailovka-Alexandrovka line, pressing the eastern front. (Source: @tass_agency/382637)
Ukrainian SBU struck Vtorovo pipeline dispatch station in Vladimir Oblast, Russia, a key petroleum logistics node, marking the second such strike in one month. (Source: @UkraineNow/82220)

Ukrainian forces hit the Titan-Barrikady defense plant in Volgograd with FP-5 Flamingo missiles, damaging a facility producing launchers and components for Iskander-M, Yars, and Topol-M systems. (Source: @eRadarrua/98338)
Russian MoD reported 511 Ukrainian aircraft-type drones and 3 Flamingo cruise missiles shot down in 24 hours, with 175 drones downed during daytime alone. (Source: @tass_agency/382639)
A prisoner exchange returned 160 Ukrainian military personnel home; Zelensky noted 1,596 returns this year and over 9,500 total since the full-scale invasion began. (Source: @UkraineNow/82206)
Ukrainian Commander-in-Chief Syrsky stated Russia plans to increase FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day by the end of 2026. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31193)
Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga assessed that Belarus is not expected to enter the war, claiming Ukraine would mirror any Belarusian involvement. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31191)
sections.threat_assessment: The conflict remains active across multiple axes with significant escalation in long-range strikes. Ukrainian forces are increasingly targeting Russian defense industrial infrastructure deep inside Russia, striking the Titan-Barrikady plant in Volgograd and the Vtorovo pipeline node in Vladimir Oblast. Russia claims continued territorial gains in the east, including Novoselovatoe and the Velykomihailovka-Alexandrovka line, while intensifying strikes on Ukrainian locomotives and gas stations with Geranium drones. The reported Russian plan to scale FPV drone usage from 7,000 to 33,000 per day signals a major anticipated increase in attrition warfare. Ukraine's loss of a MiG-29 over Poltava Oblast, though the pilot survived, highlights continued pressure on Ukrainian air assets.
sections.regional_response: President Zelensky highlighted that over 9,500 Ukrainians have been returned from captivity since the start of the war, with 1,596 returned this year, signaling sustained diplomatic engagement on prisoner exchanges. Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiga assessed that Belarus will not enter the war, suggesting Kyiv believes Minsk can be deterred. Finland has permitted the import and transit of nuclear weapons through its territory for national defense purposes, though it has no plans for permanent deployment, signaling a shift in Nordic security posture. Three Polish politicians declined Ukrainian awards amid deteriorating bilateral relations, and a dispute over a Ukrainian order awarded to a Polish figure escalated into public controversy involving references to historical crimes.
sections.intelligence_notes: The SBU's repeated strikes on the Vtorovo pipeline station in Vladimir Oblast within one month suggest a deliberate Ukrainian campaign targeting Russian petroleum logistics, corroborated by Russian civilian reports of fuel queues and collapsed fuel supplies in multiple cities. Russian claims of shooting down 511 drones in 24 hours, if accurate, indicate extremely high Ukrainian drone activity, though such figures require independent verification. The Russian MoD's claim that Ukraine is publicly claiming fuel difficulties in border regions to mislead Russian targeting suggests Ukraine's information operations are having an observable effect on Russian operational decision-making. The reported dispatch of a MiG-31K Kinzhal carrier from Savaysleyka warrants monitoring for potential long-range strike operations against high-value targets.
